2606.10428.title: Which LoRA? An Empirical Study on the Effectiveness of LoRA Techniques During Multilingual Instruction Tuning
2606.10428.paper_summary: We investigate whether commonly available LoRA variants have an advantage over basic LoRA in multilingual instruction tuning. Experiments involving LoRA and four other variants on two datasets across diverse target languages show that there is no significant advantage in using more complex LoRA variants instead of basic LoRA, with respect to balancing cross-lingual transfer and knowledge retention. An analysis of hidden embeddings reveal that layer-wise language representation remains largely similar across LLMs fine-tuned with different LoRA techniques, suggesting that architectural novelty of LoRA techniques may not translate into better cross-lingual adaptation.
2606.10428.paper_summary_zh: 我們探討常見的 LoRA 變體在多語言指令調整中是否相較於基本 LoRA 有優勢。<lb/>涉及 LoRA 和其他四個變體的實驗在兩個數據集上進行，涵蓋多種目標語言，顯示使用更複雜的 LoRA 變體相較於基本 LoRA 在平衡跨語言轉移和知識保留方面並沒有顯著優勢。<lb/>對隱藏嵌入的分析顯示，使用不同 LoRA 技術微調的 LLM 在層級語言表示上大致相似，這表明 LoRA 技術的架構新穎性可能無法轉化為更好的跨語言適應。
2606.10428.author: Thamali Wijewardhana et.al.
2606.10428.authors: Thamali Wijewardhana, Napoleon H. Reyes, Surangika Ranathunga
